description: The play pause button doesn't seem to reflect the actual state of the audio. And then also these three buttons off to the right side, we can just entirely get rid of those. Let's also get rid of the skip forward button. Since we're disallowing skip forward and also scroll until they've fully heard the one side. so no option to skip forward and no option to scroll down to the next viewpoint no option also to scrub into the future and finally the text should I think it should probably be expandable But I think for now I'm okay with just hiding the text entirely.

results.identified_issue: Play/pause was driven by a hand-set isPlaying flag that did not track AVPlayer.rate/timeControlStatus or TTS pause/finish, and every feed card mirrored the shared player flag so non-current cards looked like they were playing. Skip-forward, free scrubbing, free vertical advance, the right-edge meta rail (side / confidence / audio-type), and bottom answer text all let users leave a viewpoint before hearing it. Fix: observe rate + end-of-item + TTS delegate for honest isPlaying; isPlaying only on the current card; track farthestProgress and clamp seek/scrub to it; drop skip-forward; hide side rail and answer body; grow the scroll stack only through the first unfinished clip so next viewpoints unlock after a full listen. Assumptions: skip-back stays (only forward was banned); expandable text deferred (hidden for now as requested); fullyHeardIDs is in-memory per session not persisted. Unverified on device: fling-scroll edge cases, TTS duration estimate accuracy for the unlock gate, and whether finish-to-unlock feels right on short clips.
results.summary: Fixed play/pause state and removed skip-ahead paths on Discover
results.detailed_description:
Addressed the Discover transport issues from your voice note.

What was wrong with play/pause: the icon followed a boolean we set ourselves, not what AVPlayer or speech synthesis was actually doing—and every card in the feed shared that flag, so off-screen cards looked “playing” too. It now watches player rate / time-control status and TTS pause-continue-finish, and only the current card shows play vs pause.

What you can’t do anymore (by design):
• Skip-forward control is gone (skip-back 15s stays).
• Scrubbing cannot jump past the furthest point you’ve already heard.
• You can’t scroll down to the next viewpoint until the current one has been heard all the way through; past clips stay reachable. List mode locks upcoming rows the same way.
• The three right-side pills (side / confidence / audio-vs-text) are removed.
• Answer body text on the card is hidden for now (title + question subtitle remain). Expandable text can come later if you still want it.

Please try: play/pause should match what you hear; try dragging the scrubber ahead (should refuse); try swiping to the next side before the clip ends (should stay put); after a full listen, next card should unlock.
